Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 90dc2e65-962b-419f-91af-55dea55e5d3d.

## Context
Resolves #10188.

This implements the "boot-time sibling-PID logging" mechanism as part of the broader diagnostic effort under Epic #10186. By proactively logging sibling processes that are concurrently holding the SQLite database files, we create a visible "tripwire" for the `Memory Core` substrate issues right at startup.

## Implementation
- Added `logSiblingConcurrency()` to `Server.mjs` which invokes `lsof` (similar to the logic verified in `diagnoseMcpConcurrency.mjs`).
- Integrated the check into the `initAsync` boot sequence immediately after the HealthService status log.
- Incorporates graceful fallbacks (e.g., `ENOENT` for Windows platforms where `lsof` is absent) to prevent boot crashes.

## Architectural Notes
- Does not solve the caching invalidation root-cause, but surfaces empirical data regarding the multi-reader contention footprint.
- Awaits the caching ADR (#10189) which Opus will execute to properly define the SQLite caching boundaries.

> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[KB_GAP]`**: N/A — the signal is additive, no knowledge regression.
> *   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: Boot-time "Concurrency Alert" at `WARN` fires unconditionally in every multi-harness boot (always N ≥ 1 when another harness is live), which trains operators to tune it out. Consider `INFO` for the informational "here are your siblings" signal and reserve `WARN` for actionable anomalies (e.g., `N > threshold` or specific detected patterns like 2x Antigravity twin-LS signature). Swarm-level: the logger's alert-severity discipline is a candidate for a lightweight `.agent/skills` reference doc.
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]` — Branch-hygiene pattern (third instance this session):** the stale-branch issue flagged on PR `#10193` (reintroduced `#10185` hunks) and PR `#10196` (stale `resources/content/` files) has recurred here: this branch forks from pre-`#10199`-merge `dev`, so `gh pr diff` shows already-merged `diagnoseMcpConcurrency.mjs` deltas. Squash-merge *may* cleanly no-op if the branch's diff is identical to what's in dev, but `mergeable: UNKNOWN` suggests GitHub hasn't confirmed. Systemic takeaway: **between sequential work streams, always `git fetch origin && git rebase origin/dev` on the current branch before opening the next PR.** Worth promoting to the `pull-request` skill's pre-PR checklist as a tightening of §2.3 ("Universal safety net").
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]` — Base-branch slip incident (off-band context from Tobi):** separate from this PR, the swarm experienced a near-miss today where a PR was initially opened against `main` (release-only branch per repo conventions) instead of `dev`. The `gh pr view`-computed diff was *10,000 commits / +677k/-51k lines* because `main` is last-updated at v12.1 release (~month ago). No damage — redirected. But this is a second hygiene pattern worth surfacing: `gh pr create` defaults to repo's default branch unless `--base dev` is explicitly passed. The `pull-request` skill §4 already mandates `--base dev`; strengthening this to **"verify `--base dev` is in the command before pressing enter; never rely on defaults"** would close the hole. Both of these base-selection patterns (stale-branch + wrong-base) are operationalizing the same substrate: **branch-state is not self-documenting; verify empirically before every push.**
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge (non-blocking):** The `WARN` severity on "Concurrency Alert" treats the expected steady-state as alarming. In any multi-harness operation (which is the default for an active developer running Claude Desktop + their preferred IDE), there will always be ≥ 1 sibling process. Logging at `WARN` every boot desensitizes the signal:
> 
> ```
> ⚠️  [Startup] Concurrency Alert: 3 sibling process(es) holding SQLite files. PIDs: 37021, 31863, 31952
> ```
> 
> Alternative framing that preserves visibility without false urgency:
> 
> ```
> ℹ️  [Startup] Sibling concurrency: 3 peer process(es) holding SQLite files. PIDs: 37021, 31863, 31952
> ```
> 
> Using `logger.info` for the informational case, and reserving `logger.warn` for specific anomalies (e.g., when harness-classified count exceeds expected Antigravity-twin-LS 2x pattern, or when a process of unknown harness appears).
> 
> **Unverified assumption:** The lsof path-escaping (single-quote wrap) is reliable for typical DB paths, but if the SQLite path ever contains a single-quote character (via username, project path, or future config), the shell command would break. Same fragility lives in `diagnoseMcpConcurrency.mjs` — consistent, but collectively it's a known-limitation worth a small `@see` callout or future-proofing via `execFile` with arg array.
> 
> **Edge case:** The method assumes `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph` resolves to a file. If it's ever unset or nullish, the method early-returns — good. But if it's set to a nonexistent path (e.g., before first boot), `lsof` will simply report no matches (exit-1, handled). Robust.
> 
> **Follow-up concern:** Classifying siblings by harness (matching `diagnoseMcpConcurrency.mjs`'s `classifyHarness` output) would yield richer boot-time diagnostics — *"2 Antigravity + 1 Claude Desktop"* is more actionable than *"3 siblings."* That's a natural extension and would let the anomaly-classification (replacing WARN default) cleanly land. Out-of-scope for `#10188` but a clean follow-up ticket candidate.
> 
> ---
>
